summary: An old-school, transition-first 4-4-2 that prefers direct play and stopping attacks by any means. A team the author has a soft spot for.
style: Old-school 4-4-2 — two big centre-forwards, wingers & fullbacks combining to cross, direct & long, transition over possession.

counterReliance.note: Transition over possession (29th in avg possession) — direct & long

strengths: Two big strikers as a direct target
Wingers & fullbacks combine well to cross
Stop attacks by fouling — 1st in fouls & yellows

keyPlayers.name: Esmir Bajraktarević
keyPlayers.note: Direct dribbler, creative with an eye for goal — created 6 chances & 8 successful dribbles vs Italy

tactics: Go direct to the two strikers, combine wide to cross, and break in transition rather than build. They will happily foul to kill momentum. Niko Katić at CB and Sasa Papac on the staff add to the appeal.
sources: John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
